title: IT2 Reportable employer superannuation contributions 2024
description: Complete question IT2 to include any reportable employer superannuation contributions you received.
keywords: Superannuation, Individual tax return instructions, income test, reportable employer super, super contributions, IT2, 2024, paper tax return, paper form, paper return, paper lodgement

content_blocks.text: Reportable employer superannuation contributions are additional to the compulsory contributions your employer must make. An example of a reportable employer superannuation contribution is a contribution made on your behalf under a salary sacrifice arrangement. For more information, see Reportable super contributions .
Did your employer make reportable employer superannuation contributions on your behalf?

items: No – Write 0 (zero) at question IT2 – label T , then go to question IT3 Tax-free government pensions or benefits 2024 .

content_blocks.text: What you need to answer this question

You will need each income statement, PAYG payment summary – individual non-business, PAYG payment summary – foreign employment and PAYG payment summary – business and personal services income you received that shows an amount of reportable employer superannuation contributions.

If your payment summary or income statement shows a reportable employer superannuation contributions amount, and you did not get your employer to make superannuation contributions in addition to their compulsory superannuation contributions, you should contact your employer before lodging your return to check that the figure is correct.

If your employer has incorrectly calculated the reportable employer superannuation contributions amount, ask them to provide you with a revised payment summary or income statement showing the correct amount.
To complete this question, follow steps 1 and 2 below.
Add up the reportable employer superannuation contributions amounts shown on your:
Write the total at question IT2 – label T .

content_text:
## Things you need to know
Reportable employer superannuation contributions are additional to the compulsory contributions your employer must make. An example of a reportable employer superannuation contribution is a contribution made on your behalf under a salary sacrifice arrangement. For more information, see Reportable super contributions .
Did your employer make reportable employer superannuation contributions on your behalf?
- No – Write 0 (zero) at question IT2 – label T , then go to question IT3 Tax-free government pensions or benefits 2024 .
- Yes – Read on.
## What you need to answer this question
You will need each income statement, PAYG payment summary – individual non-business, PAYG payment summary – foreign employment and PAYG payment summary – business and personal services income you received that shows an amount of reportable employer superannuation contributions.
If your payment summary or income statement shows a reportable employer superannuation contributions amount, and you did not get your employer to make superannuation contributions in addition to their compulsory superannuation contributions, you should contact your employer before lodging your return to check that the figure is correct.
If your employer has incorrectly calculated the reportable employer superannuation contributions amount, ask them to provide you with a revised payment summary or income statement showing the correct amount.
## Completing your tax return
To complete this question, follow steps 1 and 2 below.
### Step 1
Add up the reportable employer superannuation contributions amounts shown on your:
- payment summaries
- income statements.
### Step 2
Write the total at question IT2 – label T .
## Where to go next
- Go to question IT3 Tax-free government pensions or benefits 2024 .
- Return to main menu Individual tax return instructions 2024 .
- Go back to question IT1 Total reportable fringe benefits amounts 2024 .
